rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute febrile myalgia with marked functional decline in an older adult living alone, where infection cannot be excluded remotely, warrants urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3) alongside stopping the likely culprit drug — escalating to T4 (ED) if any sepsis danger sign appears. Home self-management alone is unsafe here.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rising fever (>39C), tachycardia, an emerging infection source, or feeling much worse
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your fever climbs above 39C, you develop an obvious infection, or you feel much worse, go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Confusion, drenching rigors, breathlessness, chest pain, or collapse
escalation_edges.rationale: Danger signs of severe sepsis/organ dysfunction requiring ambulance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you become confused, get drenching shakes, become breathless, get chest pain, or collapse, call 000 straight away.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Older adult living alone with acute weakness
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for in-person assessment and arrange someone to stay overnight.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Please stop the hydralazine and let your prescriber know — it's the likely cause. But because you have a fever, feel very weak and live alone, you need to be seen in person today to rule out an infection. Use paracetamol for the aches, have your daughter stay with you, and call 000 if you become confused, breathless, or much worse.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person assessment (GP/urgent care/ED); 000 for danger signs
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend ED tonight if you cannot be seen same-day or any danger sign appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of probable hydralazine-induced lupus with fever and functional decline; hydralazine ceased pending prescriber confirmation and antihypertensive substitution; same-day in-person assessment advised to exclude infection; sepsis safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Attributing everything to a drug reaction and advising home management with NSAIDs, missing sepsis.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An early sepsis is mislabelled DILE; the patient, alone, deteriorates into septic shock overnight.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: T3 is correct; risk is failing to escalate on danger signs.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance without any danger sign is over-triage; same-day in-person assessment (T3) or ED (T4) suffices unless danger signs appear.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot obtain observations, examine, or blood-test remotely to separate drug-induced lupus from infection.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Stop the culprit drug AND arrange same-day in-person assessment to exclude infection, with a sepsis safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Attribute to DILE and advise home self-management with NSAIDs.
